outcome_contract.why: Fresh deployments and CI builds fail silently (500 on every page) when the content generation step is skipped. The materializer warning also means even manual re-runs are unsafe without the trustStrip fix.
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: The Caryina app builds and serves content on fresh checkout without a separate manual generation step; all pages fall back to hardcoded safe content rather than throwing a 500 when the file is absent or malformed.

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: No visual change; content is copy/text only
Currently: 500 error page on missing file — no graceful state
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Missing file = entire shop offline; no empty state, no degraded mode, no helpful message
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Yes — define fallback UX: which content sections get hardcoded defaults vs redirect to error page

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: File is static JSON with no secrets; no auth involved
Currently: error thrown propagates as unhandled 500 with no structured log
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: No logging when file is missing or malformed; operator has no visibility into which deployment is running stale content
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Yes — add a warning log when falling back to defaults

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: `contentPacket.test.ts` only covers chrome defaults path; no tests for missing/invalid file
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Zero coverage of error paths; fallback logic once added needs unit tests

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Two-copy divergence (root vs app-local); materializer schema missing `trustStrip`; schema drift between generator and consumer
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: App-local copy is stale and diverged; regenerating from materializer would drop `trustStrip`; need to resolve before prebuild can be wired
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Yes — resolve which path is canonical; port `trustStrip` to materializer or accept manual-only for that field

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Currently: if file is absent, every SSR request fails and throws; no cache or retry
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Single point of failure with no degraded mode
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Yes — fallback ensures reliability; prebuild ensures freshness

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: File is committed; regeneration is manual; no prebuild guard
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: If materializer run produces bad output, it breaks deployed app silently; no rollback mechanism other than reverting the JSON commit
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Yes — prebuild should be idempotent; committed baseline is implicit rollback

open_operator_questions: Should the stale `apps/caryina/data/shops/caryina/site-content.generated.json` be deleted from git as part of this work?

stage_payload.scope_summary: The Caryina Next.js app requires `data/shops/caryina/site-content.generated.json` to be present at runtime. Every page in the shop calls `readPayload()` from `apps/caryina/src/lib/contentPacket.ts`, which throws a hard `Error` if the file is missing or structurally invalid. The file is manually generated using a `scripts/` CLI — there is no `prebuild` hook to regenerate it automatically. On a fresh checkout or a CI runner that has not run the generation step, every server-rendered page throws a 500. This work makes the app resilient: the file should be auto-generated before build, and the app
goals: Add a `prebuild` step to `apps/caryina/package.json` that regenerates `site-content.generated.json` from the committed content-packet before `next build` runs.
Ensure the app never throws an unhandled runtime error for a missing or malformed file — serve hardcoded fallbacks instead.
Resolve the two-copy divergence (root `data/` vs `apps/caryina/data/`) to a single authoritative path.
Add a test that verifies `readPayload()` falls back gracefully when the file is absent.
non_goals: Migrating all content to a DB or CMS.
Internationalising the fallback copy (fallback can be EN-only).
constraints: The file is committed to git (both paths tracked); the materializer has a manual warning not to re-run it yet (`productPage.trustStrip` not ported). The prebuild step must only run the materializer when a content-packet exists and the warning condition is resolved — or must use the committed file as the baseline.
The `apps/caryina` build uses `next build --webpack`; no Turbopack edge case concerns here.
CI does not run the materializer (`caryina.yml` `build-cmd` contains no generation step).
assumptions: The materializer warning (`productPage.trustStrip` not yet ported) means a blind prebuild run would strip content. The safest prebuild is one that **only regenerates if the source hash has changed** — or the plan must first resolve the trustStrip gap in the materializer before wiring up the prebuild.
The committed `data/shops/caryina/site-content.generated.json` (repo root) is the primary copy used by the app in production (it is resolved first by `GENERATED_PAYLOAD_CANDIDATES`).
The `apps/caryina/data/` copy is an older diverged copy still tracked in git — it differs by the presence of a stale `chrome` key that was removed from the main copy.
key_entry_points: `apps/caryina/src/app/[lang]/layout.tsx` — calls `getChromeContent()` on every page render; first point of 500 failure

analysis_readiness.Blocking items: none (the one open question about the stale copy deletion defaults to "delete" and is low-risk)

rehearsal_issues.Scope Area: Materializer output schema vs consumer schema drift
rehearsal_issues.Issues Found: [Data] [High]: `trustStrip` in consumer not emitted by generator — requires porting before prebuild can be wired
[Data] [Medium]: app-local copy is stale and tracked in git
rehearsal_issues.Resolution Required: Operator decision on deletion — defaulted to delete

rehearsal_issues.Issues Found: [Reliability] [High]: CI builds succeed but deployed app crashes on any page
[Rollout] [Low]: committed file serves as implicit rollback

process_topology.end_condition: App is deployed to Cloudflare Worker

areas.label: Content generation
areas.flow: Operator manually runs `pnpm --filter scripts startup-loop:materialize-site-content-payload -- --business HBAG --shop caryina`. Result written to `data/shops/caryina/site-content.generated.json`. File is committed.
areas.issues: Warning in JSON: "Do not run materializer without porting productPage.trustStrip first." Materializer does not emit trustStrip; file was extended manually post-generation.

areas.label: Two-copy divergence
areas.flow: Two copies tracked in git: `data/shops/caryina/site-content.generated.json` (repo root, used by app in production) and `apps/caryina/data/shops/caryina/site-content.generated.json` (app-local, older, contains stale `chrome` key).
areas.issues: Copies have diverged. App-local copy has stale `chrome` key that was removed from main copy and from `contentPacket.ts`.

areas.label: App startup / runtime
areas.flow: On every server render, `readPayload()` in `contentPacket.ts` tries three candidate paths. If none exist: throws `Error("Missing generated site-content payload...")`. If file exists but is invalid: throws `Error("Invalid generated site-content payload...")`.
areas.issues: Hard throw on both missing and invalid. No fallback. 500 propagates to every page that calls any `get*Content()` function.

areas.label: CI build
areas.flow: `caryina.yml` calls `pnpm exec turbo run build --filter=@apps/caryina^...` then `opennextjs-cloudflare build`. No generation step before either. Relies entirely on committed file.
areas.issues: If committed file is stale or missing, build succeeds but deployed app serves 500 on every page.

areas.label: No prebuild hook
areas.issues: Generation is entirely manual, undiscoverable from the build command.
